content.text: One name, one effect, everywhere. The paperwork is the mercy.
Default duration: a condition lasts until the end of the afflicted's next activation. Reapplication refreshes the duration — it never stacks. The one exception is Seared, which carries a number.
Each condition lists its own exit. Beyond those: Calm Voice handles morale, and the Sin Eater can eat anything (see Sin Eater).

rows: −1 accuracy on all attacks; −1 on contested Checks. The nerves answering first.
Default duration; a Stim sheds it (see Consumables & Ammo).
At the start of your activation, take X direct damage, then reduce X by 1.
Counts itself down; an Antidote ends it (see Consumables & Ammo).
Spend 2 AP to recover, or win the contest that holds you.
No movement; no attacks beyond Adjacent.
While inside the zone: −1 accuracy, and moving toward the suppressor costs +1 AP per band.
It's the zone, not you — leave it or outlast it.
The marking source's allies gain +1 accuracy against you.
The weapon doesn't fire until cleared.
1 AP to clear; Jam Drill makes it free.
Helpless. The stabilize window is running, and any damage to you fires the Fate Roll immediately.

content.text: Seared is the exception to no-stacking: it is numbered, and reapplication can build X up to its cap of 3.
learn the exits before you need them. nobody reads tables while on fire.

Conditions are temporary by design. Contractors experiencing a condition that fails to resolve on schedule should consult the nearest Sin Eater, street doc, or actuary, in ascending order of finality.
